forecast-period.text: A weak ridge of high pressure will remain along the coast during the next few days.
For latest warnings go to www.bom.gov.au, subscribe to RSS feeds, call 1300 659 210* or listen for warnings on relevant TV and radio broadcasts.
* Calls to 1300 numbers cost around 27.5c incl. GST, higher from mobiles or public phones.

Showers mainly about the pastoral districts, possibly extending into the northern agricultural area. Possible light showers about western coasts. Possible morning fog patches about the southeast. Maximum temperatures below average, grading to near average about the southeast. Generally south to southeasterly winds, fresh at times about the coast and northeast.

Showers mainly about the pastoral districts, possibly extending into the northern agricultural area. Possible morning fog patches about the agricultural area. Maximum temperatures below average in the north, grading to near average in the south. Generally south to southeasterly winds, with local afternoon sea breezes.

Showers mainly about the pastoral and Flinders districts. Possible light showers about western coasts. Maximum temperatures below average in the north, grading to near average in the south. Generally south to southeasterly winds, with local afternoon sea breezes.

text.p: Possible showers about the pastoral districts. Maximum temperatures below average in the north, grading to slightly above average in the south. Southeast to easterly winds, with local afternoon sea breezes.

Cumulative rainfall totals to the end of Sunday: less than 2 mm about western and southern coasts, remaining dry about the Lower South East. Less 5 mm about the northern agricultural area and North West Pastoral district, increasing to 5 to 15 mm about the Flinders and North East Pastoral district and to 15 to 50mm about the northeast.
